title: cerastium holosteoides subsp. glandulosum
intro: Cerastium holosteoides subsp. glandulosum, also known as a type of toadflax or sticky mouse-ear, is a plant species that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Cerastium holosteoides subsp. glandulosum are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Cerastium holosteoides subsp. glandulosum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
